note: WF2 (Product Recommendation) and WF3 (Referral Guidance) are not independent process entities. They activate and complete within a single turn and have no sustained lifecycle. They are behavioral steps inside ConversationSession.

process_entities.name: ConversationSession
process_entities.represents: The full lifecycle of a multi-turn interaction between a user and the assistant. Container for all conversation state: current turn, DecisionContext, and history. All behavioral workflows execute inside it.

states.name: created
states.meaning: Session record exists. User identity and session ID established.

states.name: initializing
states.meaning: User profile, school, ConversationHistory, and PurchaseHistory being loaded from DB. DecisionContext being pre-seeded.

states.name: active
states.meaning: Session is processing a turn. A user message has been received and is being handled.

states.name: idle
states.meaning: Turn completed. Session is waiting for the next user message.

states.name: closed
states.meaning: Session has ended. No further turns will be processed. Terminal state.

transitions.trigger: session init begins: load user profile, school, conversation history, purchase history
turn failed mid-execution (any uncaught exception) — rollback via fail_turn(); no event emitted; user can retry

responsibilities: Own the conversation lifecycle from creation to close
Hold and provide access to the current DecisionContext
not_responsible_for: Routing to workflows — that is application orchestration

domain_events.name: SessionStarted
domain_events.emitted_when: Session moves from initializing to idle (ready for first turn)

domain_events.name: TurnProcessed
domain_events.emitted_when: Session moves from active to idle (turn completed)

process_entities.name: DecisionContext
process_entities.represents: The progressively built, structured understanding of user preferences, constraints, and fundraising intent. Starts pre-seeded with the user's school. Grows each turn. Its state is the primary input for retrieval query construction.

states.name: empty
states.meaning: No conversation signals yet. Only school anchor present (pre-seeded). Not sufficient for recommendation.

states.name: partial
states.meaning: Some preference signals accumulated. Gaps still exist. Clarification may be needed.

states.name: ready
states.meaning: Sufficient signals to construct a meaningful retrieval query and generate a recommendation.

states.name: enriched
states.meaning: Multiple turns have added depth. Retrieval queries are highly precise.

transitions.trigger: first PreferenceSignal extracted from user message
gap evaluation passes — sufficient signals for recommendation
further signals continue to improve retrieval precision

process_entities.note: Once ready, context never regresses. Signals accumulate monotonically.
responsibilities: Track identified gaps and determine when context is ready
Expose current state as the primary input for RetrievalQuery construction
Anchor fundraising context via the pre-seeded school
not_responsible_for: Constructing the retrieval query — that is an application-layer operation

domain_events.name: ContextUpdated
domain_events.emitted_when: A new PreferenceSignal is added and state changes

domain_events.name: GapDetected
domain_events.emitted_when: Gap evaluation finds the context insufficient for recommendation

domain_events.name: ContextReady
domain_events.emitted_when: Context transitions to ready state for the first time

entity_relationship.description: ConversationSession owns DecisionContext. During initialization, ConversationSession creates and pre-seeds DecisionContext. Each turn updates DecisionContext state. DecisionContext.state determines whether WF2 activates.
entity_relationship.state_drives: DecisionContext.state → drives → RetrievalQuery construction → RAG pipeline (WF2 / WF3)
